messages.message:
Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(2 inline comments)

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I'd prefer if we reduce the number of iterations for ZFS instead of skipping the test entirely.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/18465/

Patch Set 2: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/18465/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 2: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(1 inline comment)

If the patch needs to be refreshed, please address the inline review comment.

In addition, in manual test run (7 OSTs on one physical disk) with SLOW=no, compilebench still took 7760s to be finished. I'm not sure whether we need reduce the values of cbench_IDIRS and cbench_RUNS for ZFS even with SLOW=no.

Let's wait for the autotest result.

Patch Set 6: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

i've been wondering would it be possible to reconfigure for some tests? say, for parallel-scale we could put all OSTs into a single ZFS pool as we don't need to freeze the device separately (read-only support).

Patch Set 6:

> i've been wondering would it be possible to reconfigure for some tests? say, for parallel-scale we could put all OSTs into a single ZFS pool as we don't need to freeze the device separately (read-only support).

Yes, it's possible to unmount the Lustre filesystem, reformat the OSTs with the same pool name and then start the new filesystem at the beginning of parallel-scale. This also needs a change to ostdevname() in test-framework.sh. However, since not only parallel-scale hit the LU-2887, some other tests were also affected, it seems the suggestions from you and Andreas about reducing the OST count for zfs testing is more preferable.

Patch Set 6:

> However, since not only parallel-scale hit the LU-2887, some other tests were also affected, it seems the suggestions from you and Andreas about reducing the OST count for zfs testing is more preferable.

Would moving all the OSTs to a single pool help performance?

Patch Set 6: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(1 inline comment)

I think that putting the OSTs into a single pool would help to some extent. It would avoid the extra overhead of 7 separate OST pools trying to do transaction commits on LVM devices that share the same physical disk. Having just 2 or 3 OSTs would probably improve things a bit more, since there would be less overhead from Lustre per OST. 

I'm ok with changing this in the short term until the test config can change.

Patch Set 6: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Unfortunately, due to TEI-655, parallel-scale test on patch set 6 was not run, so we have no idea whether the test can pass under the autotest configuration or not. I just re-triggered the testing to get the result and flagged the ticket with -1 until the test can pass without timeout.

parents.subject: LU-4060 test: skip sanityn.sh test_74 on older than 2.4.93 MDS

commit.subject: LU-2887 tests: disable SLOW for ZFS in parallel-scale
commit.message:
LU-2887 tests: disable SLOW for ZFS in parallel-scale

Because all zfs pools are on the same physical device in autotest
ZFS backed OSTs run VERY slowly so disable SLOW.  Decrease load on zfs
backed MDS.

Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=SLOW=yes,ENABLE_QUOTA=yes mdtfilesystemtype=zfs mdsfilesystemtype=zfs ostfilesystemtype=zfs testlist=parallel-scale
Signed-off-by: Nathaniel Clark <nathaniel.l.clark@intel.com>
Change-Id: I99d980adcf4259f8431c351ce6fecb1088419130
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/7778
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
